title: Quiz: Excited States VQE
description: Test your understanding of excited state computation methods

questions.question: What is the main challenge in using VQE for excited states?

options.text: Excited states don't exist in quantum systems
Standard VQE minimization naturally converges to the ground state
Quantum computers cannot measure excited state energies

questions.explanation: VQE minimizes energy, so without additional constraints, it naturally finds the ground state (lowest energy). Special techniques like VQD or SSVQE are needed to target excited states.

questions.question: How does Variational Quantum Deflation (VQD) enforce orthogonality to previous states?

options.text: By adding overlap penalty terms to the cost function

questions.explanation: VQD adds penalty terms proportional to the squared overlap |⟨ψ|ψₖ⟩|² with previously computed states, pushing the optimization away from those states.

questions.question: What is the cost function for the (n+1)-th state in VQD?
questions.explanation: VQD cost function adds the energy ⟨ψ|H|ψ⟩ plus penalty terms βᵢ|⟨ψ|ψᵢ⟩|² for all previously computed states i < n.

questions.question: How does SSVQE differ from VQD?

options.text: SSVQE can only find the ground state
SSVQE finds multiple states simultaneously rather than sequentially
SSVQE is faster but less accurate

questions.explanation: SSVQE (Subspace-Search VQE) optimizes multiple states simultaneously by applying the same unitary to different reference states and minimizing a weighted energy sum, unlike VQD's sequential approach.

questions.question: The folded spectrum method can target excited states directly without computing all lower states.
questions.explanation: True. The folded spectrum method minimizes (H - σI)², targeting the eigenstate closest to energy σ. This allows direct targeting of any state if you have an approximate energy estimate.

questions.question: What physical quantity can be computed using ground and excited states?

options.text: Transition dipole moments and oscillator strengths

questions.explanation: Transition properties like dipole moments μ₀ₙ = ⟨0|μ̂|n⟩ and oscillator strengths require both ground and excited state wavefunctions to compute optical absorption properties.

questions.question: What is the swap test used for in VQD?

options.text: Computing the squared overlap |⟨ψ|φ⟩|² between states

questions.explanation: The swap test uses an ancilla qubit and controlled-SWAP operations to measure |⟨ψ|φ⟩|², which is needed for the overlap penalty terms in VQD.

questions.question: Which are valid applications requiring excited state calculations? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Excited states are needed for (a) absorption spectra (transitions from ground to excited), (b) photochemistry (reactions on excited state surfaces), and (d) fluorescence (emission from excited states). Ground state geometry uses only E₀.
